title: Microlicia scaberula
taxonomy: Microlicia scaberula is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. The family Melastomataceae is a large group of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome or memecylid family. This family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, including Central and South America, Africa, and Asia. Microlicia scaberula belongs to the genus Microlicia, which is a group of flowering plants within the Melastomataceae family. The classification of Microlicia scaberula is further supported by its specific morphology and anatomical characteristics.

intro: Microlicia scaberula is a species of plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the "madder family" due to its characteristic bright red flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Microlicia scaberula include flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Microlicia scaberula is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a decoction, with some preparations also incorporating the plant's leaves or roots to create tonics and infusions.
